description: Fixed k8s-e2e CI staleness at the root: decoupled the source-under-test from the kind-runner toolchain image. integration + behavioral jobs now build from a fresh `repo` git resource (`cd repo`) instead of the image's baked `/src`, so a code change is tested fresh WITHOUT a mutable-tag bump (the prior band-aid). Added `attempts: 2` to the privileged tasks to absorb transient OOM/resource-pressure retries. Validated: integration #181 built from `cd repo` (not `/src`) with no tag bump; chain green through behavioral #102/#103.
completionCaveats: Phase 3 hardening deferred as out-of-scope per spec: immutable-tag/digest pinning of the toolchain image (needs Dockerfile.kind-runner rework to drop the baked-/src build-time validation steps) and OOM pod-memory right-sizing (needs cluster-capacity data; attempts:2 already covers transient retries). Split to a dedicated hardening track if the toolchain image or cluster capacity becomes the bottleneck.
